kicker: ET LEVEL II // CHOOSING TEST FREQUENCY

beats.narration: At Level Two you set the test frequency. Two considerations usually decide it: how cleanly signals separate, and whether the test keeps up.

payload.title: PHASE & RESPONSE SPEED
payload.sub: The two calls that settle your test frequency.

beats.narration: Every variable writes its signal in its own direction. Raise the frequency, shrink the skin depth, and signals from different depths fan apart by angle.

payload.heading: Frequency sets the angle, not the size
payload.note: SHRINK δ → MORE PHASE LAG PER MM → SIGNALS FAN APART

beats.narration: So you separate by phase, never amplitude. On surfaces, park lift-off flat and alarm vertical. In tubing, the f-ninety rule spreads the wall across ninety degrees.
Second consideration: response speed. A passing defect is a brief blip on the carrier, which must finish many cycles while the flaw transits the coil.

payload.heading: The test has to keep up

beats.narration: So standardize at production speed, and re-standardize the moment speed, rotation, filters, or frequency change. Set the frequency, then prove it.

payload.sub: Phase separates it; speed lets it form.
